expectations.evidence: "Cut Q1 — ... is answered verbatim: \"Pagination: cursor-based.\"" and "Cut Q2 — ... is answered verbatim: \"600 requests/minute per token, 429 with Retry-After.\""
expectations.text: Questions 1 and 2 are explicitly cut, and the reason given is that the pasted doc already answers them.

expectations.evidence: "Keep Q4, but split it — this is the one that actually matters"
"Keep Q3 — genuine gap. ... Real question, plainly answerable, worth sending as-is."
expectations.text: Question 3 is kept, or is kept in a sharpened form.

expectations.evidence: "Two of your four are already answered in the doc they published — sending those signals you didn't read it closely." explains the cuts as findable-already and costly to send, not merely a reordering.
expectations.text: The answer explains the cut in terms of the questions not being worth the turn -- findable already, or leaving the next move unchanged -- rather than just reordering the list.
